gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/57)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/57)
text: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ الرَّجُلِ يَكُونُ لَهُ عَلَى رَجُلٍ مَالٌ قَرْضاً فَيُعْطِيهِ الشَّيْ‏ءَ مِنْ رِبْحِهِ مَخَافَةَ أَنْ يَقْطَعَ ذَلِكَ عَنْهُ فَيَأْخُذَ مَالَهُ مِنْ غَيْرِ أَنْ يَكُونَ شَرَطَ عَلَيْهِ قَالَ لَا بَأْسَ بِذَلِكَ مَا لَمْ يَكُنْ شَرْطاً .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Muhammad Bin Al Husayn, from Musa Bin Sa’adan, from Al Husayn Bin Abu Al A’ala, from Is’haq Bin Ammar,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup>, said, ‘I asked him<sup>asws</sup> about the man who happened to have wealth for himself upon a man as a loan, so he (borrower) gives something from his profits to him (lender) in fear that, that (loan) would be cut off from him. So he (the lender) takes his wealth from other than there being a stipulation (condition) upon it. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘There is no problem with that for as long as there does not happen to be a stipulation’.
en.sarwar: 3. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Muhammad ibn al-Husayn from Musa ibn Sa‘dan from al-Husayn ibn abu al-‘Ala’ from Ishaq ibn ‘Ammar who has said the following: “I once asked abu al-Hassan, Alayhi al-Salam, about the case of a man who is a debtor and pays a certain amount out of his profit to his creditor for fear of the creditor’s demand for payment, but there is no such condition between them. He (the Imam) said, ‘It is not harmful as long as it is not a condition.’”
